description: Provisioning and management of linked devices on an existing account, allowing a user to register up to a configured maximum of secondary devices that share the account identity

linking_flow: Only the primary device (device ID 1) may initiate device linking by generating a signed linking token
The linking token is single-use; attempting to reuse it must return 403 Forbidden
A new device must submit the linking token, its cryptographic keys, and its capabilities atomically; the server accepts or rejects the entire request
The primary device listens for linking completion events and may time out if the new device does not complete registration within the waiting period
key_validation: Pre-key signatures must be verified against the account's existing identity keys before the device is added; a signature failure returns 422
The new device must declare a valid set of capabilities; capabilities required for new devices are enforced and requests without them are rejected with 422
All linked devices inherit the account's identity keys; each device maintains its own pre-key bundles and registration identifiers
Capability downgrades are prevented — if the account already has a capability that requires all devices to support it, a new device without that capability cannot be linked
device_management: Linking a new device when the account has already reached its maximum device count returns 411 Device Limit Exceeded
A device may only remove itself or, in the case of the primary device, any linked device; a linked device cannot remove another linked device
The primary device cannot be deleted via the device removal endpoint
Device names are stored encrypted; the server cannot read them

given: primary device is authenticated
account has not reached its maximum linked device count
linking_token_generated.result: A signed linking token is returned to the primary device for inclusion in a provisioning message

given: primary device requests a linking token
account device count is at or above the configured maximum
device_limit_exceeded.result: HTTP 411 is returned; no token is issued until an existing linked device is removed

given: primary device has a valid linking token
new device is listening on a provisioning WebSocket address
provisioning_message_sent.result: Encrypted provisioning message is delivered to the new device via its temporary WebSocket connection

given: primary device sends provisioning message
no WebSocket subscriber is listening at the given provisioning address
provisioning_address_not_found.result: HTTP 404 is returned; the new device must re-initiate the linking flow and display a new QR code

given: new device submits link request
any signed pre-key or post-quantum last-resort key signature fails verification
invalid_prekeys.result: HTTP 422 is returned; the device is not added and must regenerate its keys before retrying

given: new device submits link request
device capabilities would downgrade a capability that requires all devices to support it
capability_downgrade.result: HTTP 409 is returned; the new device must update its software to support all required capabilities

given: new device submits link request
the capabilities field is absent or does not include all required capabilities
missing_capabilities.result: HTTP 422 is returned; capabilities are mandatory for new devices

given: new device submits a linking token that has already been consumed
token_already_used.result: HTTP 403 is returned; the primary device must generate a new token and restart the linking flow

given: new device submits valid linking token, pre-keys, registration IDs, and capabilities
account is below its device limit
device_linked.result: Device is added to the account; account and phone-number identity identifiers are returned alongside the assigned device ID

given: authenticated device requests removal of a device ID
requesting device is the primary device or is removing itself
target device ID is not the primary device ID
device_removed.result: Device record and associated keys are deleted; remaining devices receive a sync notification

errors.message: Maximum number of linked devices reached; remove an existing device before adding a new one
The new device is no longer reachable; scan the QR code again to restart the linking process
Device key signature verification failed; regenerate keys and retry
Device does not support a capability required by this account; update the app and retry
Device capability declaration is missing or incomplete
Linking token has already been used; the primary device must generate a new token

events.name: device.linking_token_issued
events.description: Primary device generated a signed linking token to begin the device provisioning flow

events.name: device.provisioning_sent
events.description: Encrypted provisioning message was delivered to the new device's temporary WebSocket address

events.name: device.provisioning_failed
events.description: Provisioning message delivery failed because no subscriber was connected at the target address

events.name: device.linked
events.description: A new device was successfully linked to the account

events.name: device.removed
events.description: A linked device was removed from the account

events.name: device.link_failed
events.description: A device linking attempt failed due to invalid keys, capabilities, or a used token

events.name: device.limit_exceeded
events.description: A linking token request was rejected because the account is at its device limit

related.reason: An account must exist with a primary device before additional devices can be linked
Each linked device uploads its own pre-key bundles during linking; senders fetch per-device keys to establish independent encrypted sessions
All devices on an account share the same identity key; safety number verification confirms the key has not changed after linking
Messages are delivered independently to each linked device over the sealed-sender path

goals.description: Provisioning and management of linked devices on an existing account, allowing a user to register up to a configured maximum of secondary devices that share the account identity

constraints.description: Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

tradeoffs.reason: authentication must prioritize preventing unauthorized access

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
